AllOtherProgramRelatedInvstGrp.Desc: LOAN TO JROX MINISTRIES, A 501(C)(3) ORGANIZATION, FOR $3,000,000 TO REFINANCE AND BUILD OUT CAMPUS.

GeneralExplanationGrp.MediumExplanationTxt: THE MISSION OF THE ED RACHAL FOUNDATION IS ESTABLISHED BY THE LAST WILL AND TESTAMENT OF THE FOUNDATION'S BENEFACTOR. IN HIS WILL DATED JUNE 2, 1964, THE LATE ED RACHAL DECREED, "I DEVISE AND BEQUEATH THE REST AND RESIDUE OF MY ESTATE OF EVERY KIND AND WHERESOEVER SITUATED REAL, PERSONAL AND MIXED, TO SUCH CORPORATION TO BE USED EXCLUSIVELY FOR THE BENEFIT OF CHARITABLE, SCIENTIFIC, LITERARY OR EDUCATIONAL PURPOSES WITHIN THE STATE OF TEXAS". IN KEEPING WITH THIS INSTRUCTION, THE ED RACHAL FOUNDATION WAS CREATED ON JULY 29, 1965. THIS RATHER PROFOUND, ALBEIT SIMPLE STATEMENT PROVIDES THE MISSION PURSUED BY THE FOUNDATION'S MANAGEMENT STAFF AND BOARD OF DIRECTORS. (WWW.EDRACHAL.ORG)THE FOUNDATION GENERATES REVENUE THROUGH ROYALTIES, RENTS, AND INVESTMENTS. COSTS INCLUDE CHARITABLE DISBURSEMENTS, OPERATIONS, AND ADMINISTRATION. DISBURSEMENTS FOR CHARITABLE PURPOSES ARE NOTED ON PART 1, LINE 26, COLUMN D WITH CONTRIBUTIONS, GIFTS, AND GRANTS PAID NOTED ON LINE 25 THEN ITEMIZED ON PART XIV SECTION 3 SUPPLEMENTAL INFORMATION.THE FOUNDATION MAKES LOANS AT BELOW MARKET INTEREST RATES TO QUALIFYING CHARITABLE ENTITIES AS REFERENCED ON PART VIII-B SUMMARY OF PROGRAM-RELATED INVESTMENTS.THE RETURN FOR THE ED RACHAL FOUNDATION INCLUDES ALL ACTIVITY OF THE BELOW LISTED CENTRAL ORGANIZATION AND SUBORDINATE ORGANIZATIONS PURSUANT TO INTERNAL REVENUE CODE SECTION 1504(E). ERF REAL ESTATE, INC., EIN 20-2661759, IS A 501(C)(2) TITLE HOLDING COMPANY ORGANIZED FOR THE EXCLUSIVE PURPOSE OF HOLDING TITLE TO REAL ESTATE, COLLECTING INCOME THEREFROM, AND TURNING OVER THE ENTIRE AMOUNT THEREOF, LESS EXPENSES, TO THE ED RACHAL FOUNDATION (EIN 74-1116595).ERF REAL ESTATE, INC. HAS BEEN GRANTED A GROUP EXEMPTION (EXEMPTION NUMBER 6431) PURSUANT TO REVENUE PROCEDURE 80-27 AND IS THE CENTRAL ORGANIZATION FOR THE FOLLOWING SUBORDINATE ORGANIZATIONS:1. ERF PORT ARANSAS INC. (EIN: 45-4468454)2. ERF TOWER II INC. (EIN: 47-4191656)3. ERF LAMAR INC. (EIN: 85-1494970)4. 802 NC INC. (EIN: 83-0802751)5. ERF WEST SIDE INC. (EIN: 85-1914948)6. ERF PALACIOS INC. (EIN: 86-3879335)7. ERF VAL VERDE INC. (EIN: 88-1331102)8. ERF INGLESIDE, INC. (EIN: 92-0512114)9. ERF LEOPARD, INC. (EIN: 92-1878901)10. ERF BUFFALO, INC. (EIN: 92-3286012)ERF FLIGHT SERVICES, LLC IS A WHOLLY OWNED SUBSIDIARY OF THE ED RACHAL FOUNDATION AND PROVIDES DAILY FLIGHT SERVICES FOR DRISCOLL CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL MEDICAL STAFF TO VISIT REMOTE CLINICS IN THE REGION. ERF FLIGHT SERVICES IS CONSIDERED AN "IGNORED OR "DISREGARDED" ENTITY FOR FEDERAL TAX PURPOSES AND IS CONSIDERED AS A PART OF THE ED RACHAL FOUNDATION. THEREFORE, THE INCOME AND EXPENSES OF THE ERF FLIGHT SERVICES, LLC ARE INCLUDED ON THE ED RACHAL FOUNDATION'S FORM 990-PF.COMPENSATION FOR THE DIRECTORS AND OFFICERS IS BASED ON RECOMMENDATIONS RECEIVED FROM LEGAL COUNSEL. LEGAL COUNSEL RETAINS QUALIFIED INDEPENDENT COMPENSATION CONSULTANTS TO REVIEW ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES AGAINST MARKET SURVEYS, SITE VISITS, AND INTERVIEWS WITH EACH DIRECTOR AND OFFICER. THE COMPENSATION CONSULTANTS PERFORM ADDITIONAL REVIEW AND ANALYSIS AS NECESSARY TO SUBSTANTIATE ITS RECOMMENDATIONS.

GeneralExplanationGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: STATEMENT - FORM 990-PF PART XIV GRANTS AND CONTRIBUTIONS
GeneralExplanationGrp.MediumExplanationTxt: INFORMATION IN COMPLIANCE WITH IRC SECTION 4945(H)(3) AND TREASURYREGULATION SECTION 53.4945-5FOR TAX YEAR 2023, THE ED RACHAL FOUNDATION ("FOUNDATION") MADE A $5,000,000 CASH GRANT TO THE LOUISE ALLEN FOUNDATION ("GRANTEE") AT 555 N. CARANCAHUA SUITE 240 CORPUS CHRISTI, TEXAS 78401 PURSUANT TO A WRITTEN GRANT AGREEMENT. THE FOUNDATION ALSO DONATED $2,330,450 OF REAL ESTATE TO BE USED FOR GRANTEE'S EXEMPT PURPOSES. THE PURPOSE OF THE GRANT IS TO FUND THE OPERATING EXPENSES AND CAPITAL IMPROVEMENTS REQUIRED TO FURTHER THE GRANTEE'S EXEMPT PURPOSE. PRIOR TO MAKING THE GRANT, THE FOUNDATION CONDUCTED AN INQUIRY TO OBTAIN ASSURANCE THAT THE GRANTEE WILL USE THE GRANT FOR INTENDED PURPOSES.THE GRANT WAS MADE SEPTEMBER 11, 2023 WITH DISTRIBUTIONS REPORTED AS FUNDS WERE EXPENDED. THE FOUNDATION RECEIVED AND VALIDATED GRANTEE'S ANNUAL REPORT ON OCTOBER 1, 2024. THE GRANTEE SPENT $1,927,597 ON SALARIES, WAGES AND BENEFITS, $470,679 ON REPAIRS AND MAINTENANCE, $365,454 ON INSURANCE, AND $901,098 ON OTHER MISCELLANEOUS EXPENSES IDENTIFIED IN GRANTEE'S ANNUAL REPORT. GRANTEE SPENT $1,454,472 ON CONSTRUCTION AND ASSETS. THE FOUNDATION HAS DIRECT ACCESS TO THE INFORMATION NECESSARY TO VERIFY THE ACCURACY OF THE GRANTEE'S REPORT. TO THE KNOWLEDGE OF THE FOUNDATION, THE GRANTEE HAS NOT DIVERTED ANY PORTION OF THE FUNDS FROM THE PURPOSE OF THE GRANT.
